description: Science, history, geography. Bring your brain. Leave your ego.

questions.hostIntro: Welcome to the nerd round. If you're already nervous, that's completely reasonable.
questions.question: What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.hostCommentary: NITROGEN at about 78%. Oxygen is only 21%. You've been breathing mostly nitrogen your whole life and didn't even know it. You're welcome.

questions.hostIntro: History class is back in session. Except this time there's no exam. Just shame.
questions.question: Which ancient wonder of the world is still standing today?
questions.hostCommentary: The Great Pyramid of Giza — the only ancient wonder still standing. It held the record for tallest structure in the world for 3,800 years. Overachiever.

questions.hostIntro: Quick geography check. You've definitely seen this on a map. Probably.
questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.hostCommentary: CANBERRA! Not Sydney. Not Melbourne. Canberra was purpose-built as the capital because Sydney and Melbourne couldn't agree on which one deserved it. Classic.

questions.hostIntro: This one requires actual knowledge. Good luck.
questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.hostCommentary: Diamond! On the Mohs hardness scale, diamond scores a 10. Graphene is stronger in different ways but diamond wins on hardness. The jewellery industry thanks you for knowing this.

questions.hostIntro: This is a history question that will make you feel either very smart or very silly.
questions.question: Which US President was never elected President or Vice President?
questions.hostCommentary: GERALD FORD! He was appointed VP when Spiro Agnew resigned, then became President when Nixon resigned. The only person to hold both offices without winning a national election. Remarkable.

questions.hostIntro: Physics time. Your brain is a muscle. Allegedly.
questions.question: What is the name of the force that keeps the nucleus of an atom together?
questions.hostCommentary: The Strong Nuclear Force! It's the strongest of the four fundamental forces, but only works over incredibly short distances. It's basically the world's most intense short-range relationship.

questions.hostIntro: Quick: which direction is your brain spinning right now? Probably the wrong one.
questions.question: Which country has the most time zones?
questions.hostCommentary: FRANCE with 12 time zones! Because of its overseas territories. Russia has 11 and is furious about it. China technically has 5 but uses 1 to project unity. France wins by being extremely spread out.

questions.hostIntro: Science words are going to fly at you. Connect them to the clue. This is the smartest you'll feel today or the dumbest. Probably both.
questions.question: ELEMENTS ON THE PERIODIC TABLE
questions.hostCommentary: Gold, Iron, Neon, Silicon, and Helium are elements. Water is a compound (H₂O), Salt is a compound (NaCl), Diamond is a form of carbon not listed alone, Ozone is O₃, and Bronze is an alloy. Chemistry: making everything complicated since forever.

questions.hostIntro: Country or capital city? Seems easy. It isn't.
questions.question: Country or Capital City?
questions.hostCommentary: Bern is the capital of Switzerland. Luxembourg is both a country AND its own capital — we gave it to Capital City. Nauru is a country. Valletta is the capital of Malta. Tuvalu is a country. Monaco is both but we called it a capital. Funafuti is the capital of Tuvalu. Vaduz is the capital of Liechtenstein. Geography is hard and we're sorry.

questions.hostIntro: True or false, smartypants edition. Sixty seconds. Up for true, down for false. Try not to implode.
questions.question: True or False? Science & History Edition!
answers: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell
Albert Einstein failed maths as a child
Water boils at 100°C at sea level
The Earth is closer to the sun in summer (in the Northern Hemisphere)
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the pyramids being built
The Eiffel Tower is taller in summer than in winter
questions.hostCommentary: Sound is much slower than light. Einstein was excellent at maths. Earth is actually CLOSER to the sun in Northern Hemisphere winter. The glass-as-liquid thing is a myth. The Eiffel Tower really does expand in heat — up to 15cm taller in summer. Sharks: 450 million years old. Trees: only 385 million. Metal.

questions.hostIntro: Something scientific is being revealed. Buzz in when you're confident. Or when you're guessing. We can't tell the difference.
questions.question: What is this famous scientific image?
questions.hostCommentary: The Pillars of Creation! Photographed by the Hubble Space Telescope in 1995 and again by the James Webb Telescope in 2022. They are columns of gas and dust in the Eagle Nebula. Gorgeous and terrifying.

questions.hostIntro: Final question. This one is genuinely hard. No shame in getting it wrong. Some shame. A little shame.
questions.question: The Hundred Years' War between England and France lasted how long?
questions.hostCommentary: 116 YEARS (1337–1453)! The Hundred Years' War was not 100 years. It was named during a period before anyone had checked a calendar. Historians have been embarrassed ever since.
